title: 8 MCU Characters More Powerful Than Thor, Ranked

blocks.content: Ever since Chris Hemsworth's Thor first appeared in the Marvel Cinematic Universe, he’s stood out as one of its strongest heroes. Sure, the Hulk can level a city, but he still has his limits when he's Bruce Banner. As the God of Thunder and wielder of Mjolnir, there's no doubt that Thor is the most powerful original Avenger. But contrary to popular belief, he isn’t even the most powerful character who showed up during Phase One of the MCU, and since then, several heroes and villains have emerged with abilities that rival or surpass his.

Some of them wield magic. Some can bend reality itself. And some of them are so strong, they've even destroyed Thor's signature weapon without breaking a sweat. After assessing every heavy hitter in the MCU, I determined which characters have what it takes to defeat the God of Thunder in battle. While the list of superpowered beings who can challenge the Norse god is short, these eight MCU characters outrank Thor with their raw power.

Why You Should Trust Me: I've seen every movie and series in the MCU, read decades of Marvel comics, and have paid close attention to the characters and their powers over the years. Since I previously covered comic book news for Screen Rant, it was practically my job to discern this type of thing. As I curated this list of characters who could defeat Thor, I compared pivotal moments throughout the characters' on-screen appearances and narrowed it down based on their abilities, wins, and defeats.

Thanos— Even without the Infinity Stones, Josh Brolin's Thanos could push the God of Thunder to his limits. For that reason, I think it's worth mentioning that the Mad Titan could handle the Asgardian in a fight, but ultimately, his power didn't compare to Thor's in Avengers: Endgame. However, Thanos certainly came the closest to putting him in the ground.

Sentry— Lewis Pullman's Sentry absolutely deserves a shout-out, because I can't imagine Thor overpowering him on his own through brute strength. In Thunderbolts, Sentry's power goes beyond reality as he takes hold of New York City. Based on his comic book source material, I know Sentry is capable of even greater feats. His dark side doesn't just cause destruction— it warps his surroundings into a living nightmare, and Thor wouldn't stand a chance against him.

Yes, Kang was defeated by ants in Ant-Man and the Wasp: Quantumania— but that was just one of his infinite variants across the vast multiverse. Although Thor is a Norse god, Kang is a human who has achieved god-like status, overseeing countless universes and uniting every multiversal variant of his in the process. Each one has different strengths, but together, they make up the Council of Kangs, an army of technologically advanced and highly intellectual supervillains. Even if Thor won a fight against one of them, he would be helpless if the Kangs came together to avenge their fallen variant. Although Thor has Stormbreaker and summons lightning, Kang can rewrite history so that Thor was never born in the first place. That's a level of power that even Norse gods rightfully fear.

Adam Warlock has an unfair advantage against a large portion of the MCU, since he was engineered by the Sovereign to be the perfect super weapon. When he first appeared in Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3, he wasn't even at the height of his abilities, but he still showcased powers that rival the God of Thunder's— flying at super speed and displaying near-invulnerability. Thor is mighty, but Warlock was designed to rise to the challenge. For that reason, I doubt Thor would be able to stop Warlock on his own. Although Thor is a god, Warlock is a cosmic being, and that isn't necessarily too different according to the rules set by the MCU.

It shouldn't come as a surprise that when Odin is operating at full power, he’s in a league above his son. Odin is the Norse God of Norse gods, so it makes sense. The MCU only hints at his true might. It was never fully shown on-screen, but the limited information we have about him is enough for us to know he would be a worthy opponent to Thor. However, if Thor is powerful, it only follows that his father would possess similar strength as the most legendary Norse god in existence.

When Odin approached death, he was still able to restrain Hela in his old age, something that Thor couldn't even do in his prime. His command over the Odinforce places him among the most powerful characters in the MCU, with the ability to manipulate reality on a cosmic level. It's safe to say that Thor would be overwhelmed in battle against Odin, even though it would take a What If-style scenario to see that face-off happen in the MCU.

When Hela was introduced in Thor: Ragnarok, she quickly overpowered Thor. The Goddess of Death shattered Mjolnir with just one hand and was barely phased by Thor's attempts to fight back. He couldn't beat her alone. Thor required the help of others, including a giant fire demon. He didn’t even possess the motivation to win in battle before the destruction of Asgard. Hela isn’t just stronger than the God of Thunder— she’s the embodiment of death itself, with power that comes straight from Asgard. If she hadn't been defeated when she was, Hela could've turned into an Avengers-level threat, and arguably, she already was, since one Avenger wasn't enough to take her down.

Doctor Strange’s mastery of the mystic arts puts him far above many of his fellow Avengers, including the God of Thunder. Strange can manipulate time, access other dimensions, project his consciousness, and more. Thor's power seems very limited in comparison. In Thor: Ragnarok, Cumberbatch's character casually overwhelmed Thor, transporting him all around the Sanctum with ease. The scene teased that Thor wouldn't be a match for Strange if the two were forced to fight each other. In Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness, Strange is even capable of crossing into the multiverse, fighting multiversal threats, and wielding forbidden magic. For that reason, I can't imagine any Norse god would be able to put up a fight against the Sorcerer Supreme. Thor may command lightning, but Strange commands the fabric of reality.

Even in comics, Captain Marvel is one of the strongest in the Marvel Universe. So, of course, she's just as much of a powerhouse in the movies. She can fly at super speed, absorb energy, and unleash photon blasts, dwarfing Thor’s lightning. In Avengers: Endgame, Captain Marvel single‑handedly destroyed a massive warship and went toe-to-toe with Thanos, almost overpowering him without using a weapon. Even Thor needed his hammer, or Stormbreaker, when he faced the Mad Titan. But in my opinion, the thing that gives Captain Marvel an edge over Thor, more than anything else, is her energy absorption. The more power she takes in, the stronger she becomes— so if Thor called down lightning to strike her, she could use it as fuel and turn it around on him. She’s a walking nuclear reactor, and even Norse gods can’t match her in battle.

These days, Loki isn't just the God of Mischief. After the events of Loki Season 2, he's the God of Stories. At this point in the MCU, he's not just a trickster— he's a cosmic guardian with dominion over the multiverse. Even before he ascended to his current status, he proved to have the edge over Thor with his magic, fighting the Avengers as their first major threat. Since then, Loki's power has only increased, and if he wanted to, I could see him manipulating timelines in similar ways to Kang or Doctor Strange, if he wanted to defeat his brother. Thor might be a Norse god, but Loki has become something even greater.

Wanda Maximoff (AKA Scarlet Witch) is the most powerful being in the MCU, and few would argue otherwise after the events of Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness. Wanda's chaos magic gives her the ability to reshape reality itself, and when she evolved into the Scarlet Witch, she became consumed by a level of magic that proved to be too much even for Doctor Strange. In WandaVision, she subconsciously created an entire reality born of her grief from losing Vision. In Multiverse of Madness, Scarlet Witch effortlessly defeated the Illuminati, which included some heavy hitters, like Captain Marvel and Professor X (Patrick Stewart). I think that terrifying sequence alone speaks for itself when putting her side-by-side with the God of Thunder. Scarlet Witch rewrites existence on a whim, which means she could erase Thor from reality with a single thought.

Thor may be one of Marvel's most iconic heroes, but the MCU has proven he isn't unbeatable. If the God of Thunder had to square up against any of these eight, I don't foresee him walking away from the fight.
